a: An error returned by the LinkPresentation framework.
Possible error values that can be returned from LinkPresentation APIs.
An error indicating that the metadata fetch was canceled by the client.
An error indicating that a metadata fetch failed.
An error indicating that the metadata fetch was not allowed due to system policies.
An error indicating that the metadata fetch took longer than allowed.
An unknown error.
The domain for Link Presentation errors.
An object that contains metadata about a URL.
A rich visual representation of a link.
An object that retrieves metadata for a URL.
Fetch, provide, and present rich links in your app.
A structure containing metadata about a URL.
Describes a kind of attachment to the metadata.
Cancels a metadata request.
Determines if a specific `Transferable` type can be loaded for an attachment in the metadata.
A code for the error.
The domain for the error.
The descriptor for an icon attachment. The corresponding media type must support the content type of the metadata’s icon data.
An object that retrieves data corresponding to a representative icon for the URL.
The descriptor for an image attachment. The corresponding media type must support the content type of the metadata’s image data.
An object that retrieves data corresponding to a representative image for the URL.
Creates a [LinkMetadata](/documentation/linkpresentation/linkmetadata) value from a [LPLinkMetadata](/documentation/linkpresentation/lplinkmetadata) instance.
Creates a [LinkMetadata](/documentation/linkpresentation/linkmetadata) value from the specified properties by fetching the specified URL.
Creates a [LinkMetadata](/documentation/linkpresentation/linkmetadata) value from the specified properties by fetching the URL using the specified request.
Initializes a link view with specified metadata.
Initializes a placeholder link view without metadata for a given URL.
Creates a new [LinkMetadata](/documentation/linkpresentation/linkmetadata) value for the specified URL.
Loads the media data of this metadata for an attachment as the specified Transferable type if possible.
The metadata from which to generate a rich presentation.
An error indicating that the metadata fetch was canceled by the client.
An error indicating that a metadata fetch failed.
An error indicating that the metadata fetch took longer than allowed.
The original URL of the metadata request.
The original URL of the metadata request.
A remote URL corresponding to a representative video for the URL
A remote URL corresponding to a representative video for the URL.
Sets the media data in the metadata for an attachment.
A Boolean value indicating whether to download subresources specified by the metadata.
Fetches metadata for the given `NSURLRequest`.
Fetches metadata for the given URL.
The time interval after which the request automatically fails if it hasn’t already completed.
A representative title for the URL.
A representative title for the URL.
The URL that returned the metadata, taking server-side redirects into account.
The URL that returned the metadata, taking server-side redirects into account.
The descriptor for a local video attachment. The corresponding media type must support the content type of the metadata’s local video data.
An object that retrieves data corresponding to a representative video for the URL.
